sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Burnley have shown a mixed bag in their last 5 matches, managing 2 draws, 1 win, and 2 losses, with a strong 5-1 victory over Millwall but struggles against top teams. Tottenham Hotspur, on the other hand, have also been inconsistent, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their recent 5 games, including narrow defeats to Bournemouth and Aston Villa. Burnley's defense remains leaky, conceding in all of their last 5 matches, while Tottenham have struggled to convert chances despite creating opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Tottenham have dominated recent encounters, winning all 5 of their last head-to-head matches against Burnley, including a 3-0 victory earlier this season and a 2-1 win in May 2024. Burnley have failed to keep a clean sheet in these games, conceding multiple goals in most encounters, which highlights Tottenham’s attacking edge over them.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Burnley, sitting 19th with 14 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 42 goals this season (19th in the league), while their attack has managed only 23 goals. Tottenham, in 14th with 27 points, have a more balanced record with 31 goals scored and 29 conceded, showing greater attacking threat. Burnley’s low possession (40.6%, 20th) and high opponent chances created (69, worst in the league) suggest they’ll struggle to control the game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Burnley’s Jaidon Anthony has been in form, averaging a 7.53 Sofascore rating with 2 goals in the last 5 matches, but key absences like Josh Cullen could hurt their midfield. Tottenham’s Cristian Romero (7.88 average rating, 2 goals in last 5) is a standout, though they miss Richarlison (7 goals this season) and other key midfielders like Mohammed Kudus. Tottenham still retain enough quality in their squad to exploit Burnley’s weaknesses.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Tottenham’s historical dominance and Burnley’s defensive frailties, the Sofascore prediction of a 55% away win probability seems plausible. Burnley’s recent home form offers some hope, but Tottenham’s superior attacking stats and head-to-head record make them favorites. Expect a competitive game with goals likely on both sides due to defensive inconsistencies.
